title: Beantech Gold Engage Plus Smartwatch for iOS and Android with 8GB of Music Storage and Leather Strap
description: Beantech Engage Plus smart Watch provides full function applications in a classically elegant design. Stay healthy by using a full array of fitness apps, then back up your data on Beantech Watch app. Send and receive phone calls, texts and emails on the Watch. Load and store over 1000 songs in the music Vault. Beantech Engage Plus works with both iPhone and Android phones. Bit Watch app available on Apple app store and Google play store at No additional cost. Polished Silver with Brown Calfskin Leather strap.
features: FUNCTION - full function smart watch in a Classic design. Ips touch screen for bright clear viewing and interfacing with pre-loaded apps. Works with iPhone or Android.
COMMUNICATIONS - send and receive phone calls, texts and email. Receives notifications from phone apps.
FITNESS- count daily steps, check your heart rate, monitor your sleep patterns and upload to Bit watch app to keep track. Compare to daily goals.
MUSIC VAULT - 8G of on board storage allows you to load and store over 1000 songs on the watch, then take them with you without your phone!
BIT WATCH APP - interfaces with Bit watch app to allow communications, sync time and store fitness data. Bit watch app is available in Apple app store and Google play store at no additional cost.
COMPATIBILITY - iPhone - iOS 7.1 or higher and model 5S or newer. Android phones - Android 4.4 or higher.

reviews.title: worth the try, gorgeous clear display, ease of use, fits my 7" wrist,
reviews.text:
It's pretty good, especially if you dont need a name brand. It has a beautifully clear display. Easy to install app and pairs well with no hassle. Has all the bells and whistles of name brand products. While I prefer fitbit due to familiarity.

I havent put it entirely through its paces. I noticed that it was registering nearly same heart rate as my fitbit sense. I havent it worn it long enough to determine blood oxygen. There is still a lot to learn. The learning curve feels a little clunky compared to my fitbit.

Has ability to connect to Alexa, take and make calls, music. Can make changes through app or scroll through the screen face.

The watch band fits my 7" wrist and feels comfortable enough. Typical silicone band. It is attached via pins, so I imagine it is possible to swap it out but not sure. I may try some old bands to see if it is doable. I'd also prefer for it to have a soft protective cover (bumper guard) to protect screen and edges.

I still prefer the ease of using a name brand, but this has a good looking interface. Main thing is to see how accurate it is. I think it has been fine for heart rate, steps, but will need more sleep time for seeing how well it tracks sleep and oxygen.

I dont rate on price, but I comment. This is pretty reasonable, but it looks like there are many similar products for less. So compared to a name brand of similar features this is a bargain. Compared to other no name generics, this is competitive. Some are easily $20 less. I noticed the price on this vacillates. I think I got it for $70, and have seen it as low as 35-40.

title: Samsung Galaxy Watch3 2020 Smartwatch (Bluetooth + Wi-Fi + GPS) International Model (Bronze, 41mm)
description: The only smartwatch with a precise rotating bezel for navigating between apps. Larger screen, but lighter and thinner for more comfort: With its subtle rotating bezel, it's easier to see and focus on what's important on an even larger screen. The Galaxy Watch3 is thinner and smaller, and up to 15% lighter with a sophisticated look that, thanks to its reduced frame, is perfect for the wrist. Breathe easier with built-in oxygen measurement: The Galaxy Watch3 uses red LEDs and infrared rays to calculate your SpO2 levels or the amount of oxygen in your blood. Because oxygen saturation is directly related to physical activity, you can use the Galaxy Watch3 to push your routine to the next level. Enjoy a better sleep: The Galaxy Watch3 records your active sleep, deep sleep, and total sleep time to keep track and improve the quality of your sleep. Samsung partnered with the National Sleep Foundation to include educational content and provide tips on your device to help you sleep better. Do your best and then repeat it: With over 120 home workout programs to choose from, the Galaxy Watch3 helps you move your body at your own pace. Pick one from your phone and stream it on your TV. It will show you your heart rate in real-time while you exercise. Use the Ridge Sport strap for even more accurate stats. Continue your conversations: When you receive a new message, the Galaxy Watch3 shows your conversation history so you can follow them. You can also view images without needing an extra touch, making it easier to keep up while you're working out. Add some context with the smart answer: Respond to messages at all times and without missing a beat. The Galaxy Watch3's smart reply analyzes the texts and photos you receive and gives you possible answers in different categories, such as food, celebrations, selfies and much more.

reviews.title: Great watch, much better than my old "smart" watch
reviews.text:
I got Moto 360 several years ago when smart watches first came out and it was nice, but annoying. It was tied into everything and kept giving me alerts and so eventually I got tired of it. My wife also had a first generation Samsung Gear watch and that was about the same as the Moto.
Well, fast forward a few years and now my wife, daughter, son and daughter-in-law all have newer smart watches (Apple Watches and Fitbit) and they all love them. It was time to get a new phone, so I got a Samsung S20 FE and then decided to get the Samsung watch. I saw on the reviews that it really works well with Samsung phones and so I decided to get the Watch 3. I was looking at the Watch 2, which had a few less features and then I found the Watch 3 in the 41mm size and then found a refurbished one so I ended up paying about the same for the Watch 3 as I would have for the Watch 2.
I'm very impressed with the watch and really love it. I didn't get the one with a cell phone, it just connects to my phone with bluetooth and/or wifi, but it's really amazing. I get my messages, and can turn off the ones that I don't want and then I also get all the health apps. So, now it tells me to get off my chair and walk around and does EKG and stress tesing and Oxygen levels..
I was concerned that the 41 mm might be too small, but it is a great size for me as you can see from the pic. I have had a Citizen Echo Drive for more than 10 years and it is a little larger, but I like the size of the Watch 3 better.
If you are thinking about getting a watch, this one is a good deal right now and I believe it does work best if you use it with a Samsung phone.

reviews.title: Love the watch, hate the battery.
reviews.text: I don't use the sleep tracking because the watch is bulky and annoying while trying to sleep. The mobile app is ok, constantly running in background.  The battery life sucks, only good for a day and sometimes a day and a half which is to bad because I do love all the things it does. I think I can answer my phone on my watch (still figuring out how) can definitely hang up on unwanted calls and can read messages.  Good pedometer and workout tool. Overall nice watch with crappy battery life.

reviews.title: Outstanding value
reviews.text: I got this for my spouse who has an android phone.  He absolutely loves it!  He was so thrilled with it and how easy it was to set up and use that he gave me a list of the pros and cons. Pros: comfortable; stylish (never heard him use that word before lol); there's a watchface for all tastes and occasions; the features are intuitive and the charge lasts for 24+ hours.  The only cons he mentioned was that it did not give an approximate charge time and that he can't figure out how to add contacts. I've never seen him so enthusiastic about a fitness watch before and he previously disdained them. Based on his experience, I would highly recommend this one.

reviews.title: It works as a watch but most other functions are suspect
reviews.text:
This smart watch looks nice enough - someone could easily confuse it for an older Apple Watch - and its easy to make custom watch faces (I put a pic I took at an Iron Maiden show on mine). And it is jam packed with features - way more than some other watches in this price range.

Unfortunately, I have trouble trusting its more advanced features.

A number of times when taking my temperature the watch says I have hypothermia, measuring me between 87-95f. I am a 200lb man living in in Southern California and my wife won’t turn the thermostat down below 74 degrees. I don’t have hypothermia. If it can’t properly read my body temperature, there’s no way I can trust its accuracy with many of the other health features - blood oxygen, blood pressure, heart rate, etc

That step count seems pretty accurate but ONLY once I paired the watch with the Da Fit app. Before I paired the watch, it measure me taking like 350 steps while just sitting on my couch. Not sure why it would measure differently paired or unpaired but it did - significantly. I would not recommend this as a fitness tracker for someone without a smart phone (yes, there are still a few people without smart phones).

And the sleep tracker seems to at least get my sleep times accurate, though I am unsure how well it reads light/deep sleep as I don’t really trust the readings from it’s health sensors. I want to believe its doing a good job. I like the way the app presents the sleep timeline and information. But I just don’t know if its accurate because the watch thinks I have hypothermia.

As for the non-health related functions, they are pretty spotty too. I stopped receiving text notifications after a couple days and could not get them to start back up again without completely disconnecting the phone from the app and starting over again.

Pros:
- Looks like an old gen Apple Watch.
- Customizable face
- Lots of features
- Doesn’t technically *need* a smart phone for many features
- Step counter seems accurate when paired
- Great battery life for a smart watch. I wore it for 5 days without charging.
- Nice app

Cons:
- Highly inaccurate step counter when it is not paired
- Health features are unreliable
- Notifications are unreliable
- The speaker in the watch sounds terrible
- Vibration function is weak and easily missable in active situations
- Sometimes the watch doesn’t ‘wake’ from sleep as easily/instantly as it should. I find myself having to give it an extra shake to wake it almost every time.

reviews.title: Great affordable smartwatch
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:52a9c3b58a7816ac48b831c65b3aa6ca]] This is a great smartwatch at a reasonable price. It works really well, making and receiving calls, and all of the other features. It is lightweight and charges really fast. The battery life is really good too. You cannot beat the price. Great value and highly recommended.

reviews.title: Unboxing
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:53bca64d7f3459321ad803eb09c234eb]] Let's start off with what I liked. I liked that is not heavy and feels comfortable. I like the size of the overall screen of the watch. I liked that it is easy to connect to your android device. I do not like that after a free trial service of the texting and monitoring of all the apps, you have to purchase monitoring space or data. I don't like that you are limited to how many contacts you can store on the device. I do not like the overall "ability to text feature." I feel that the text input data can be improved. Down to what and how you reply via messages.

reviews.title: Great watch for a great price
reviews.text: I was skeptical about the watch as I had purchased other watches only to have them fail miserably. This watch turned out great. The most important thing to do is read the manual. Especially about how to wear the watch, which is a little higher than a normal watch. This will make the accuracy much better. The step counter is controlled by arm movement not actual steps, so if you walk stiff armed , it will be inaccurate. Move your arms when walking , the watch will be much more accurate.Make sure you go into the app on your phone and activate the exercises your interested in. This will help the watch to track it better. Also activate the media part of the app for sms,email etc. You can also monitor who is calling you, so you can choose to answer you phone or not. I am extremely pleased with my purchase and will be watching for other products from this company.

The set up was fairly easy - I received the watch in the early evening and had it set up within half an hour ( I am in my seventies). This is my 3rd watch - first was very expensive and too invasive - second watch cost twice as much as this one and quit charging in the second week - third times a charm.

reviews.title: Poor Measurements - Support Cannot Help
reviews.text:
After charging for 30 minutes as instructed, I paired the smart watch with the app. This was easy, and the one reason I gave it 2 stars instead of 1. The other reason for 2 stars, the interface was easier to use than I expected.

But if the measurements are not accurate, ease of use doesn't matter.

I bought this smart watch to track my heart rate while exercising—mainly walking. After recent heart procedures, my doctor wants me to keep my pulse below a certain rate. Each time I measure, the pulse differs substantially from the pulse oximeter that always agrees with my doctor's office pulse oximeter, and heart monitor. It goes as high as 90 when the PO says 52. I took multiple measurements, including taking my own pulse. The closest measurements were 60 when the actual rate was 51 or 52.

Support is through a chat on the app. I did not wait long for support, however they did not try to help me. They insisted that the heart rate changes. That I had to let it sync with the app. Not once did they address the possibility that the smart watch might be reading the rate wrong. Told me to try again after I had already tried multiple times.

Focusing on anything but the issue I am reporting does not help me. Even suggesting I exercise and measure my heart rate. Telling me the human heart rate is constantly changing. Literally, answers like they were talking to a small child.

I had high hopes for this device. It disappointed on two levels - Heart Rate, Support. I cannot recommend. Look for a different smart watch if you need accurate heart rate measurements.

reviews.title: NOT AS DESCRIBED AT ALL
reviews.text: I carefully read all reviews before purchasing this smart watch but was so disappointed. It would not hold preferences & kept defaulting to kg & cm. Would not toggle from one thing to another on app. Had to go all the way out of app & go back in before it would toggle from steps to profile to anything else. Sometimes I'd get my cell to ring on my watch, never could answer it or text back from the watch. I'd rather pay the extra & get an apple watch that actually works. I can't tell you how many times I set the preferences & saved them only to find it defaulted right back the next day.  One of the sorriest watches I've ever owned & I kept it 2 weeks before returning it really trying to make it work.

reviews.title: Better than name brand. Long battery life!!
reviews.text: I will take this watch over a fit bit any day! In fact my husband bought me a fit bit and I returned it because thus watch is soooo much better. What I like most is the BATTERY LIFE IS AMAZING! I bought it weeks ago and have only charged it twice.

reviews.text: I really like this watch, has everything I wanted and everything works with one exception:  the sleep analysis feature is worthless.  It only records sleep between 10:00 pm and 8:00 am and if you move or get up it screws up everything. Good customer service, they really try to help, but they will tell you if you get up to go to the bathroom, watch begins recording all over again. Too bad!

reviews.title: Glitchy smartwatch has a mind of its own
reviews.text:
I've been using this smartwatch for about five weeks. There are tons of watch faces to choose from, but I couldn't find any with just the info I wanted at a glance: time, date, steps, and weather. I found one watch face with everything but weather and loaded that, but the watch face spontaneously changes several times a day to designs I've never selected. I don't like having to reset it with my phone every few hours to get back to my preferred watch face.

It vibrates at random times and shows me (what I believe is) some sort of prompt to reset it. Sometimes it prompts me for voice commands or makes phone calls for no apparent reason. I erased my contacts, so I hope it won't accidentally wrist-dial anyone else.

It needs to be recharged every 2-3 days — not every ten days as claimed for "Bluetooth daily usage mode" — and I'm NOT using the majority of its supposed features.

I've reached out to customer support a few times, but they say I'm doing something wrong. I'm just wearing it; the glitches happen whether I'm moving or still. Support has been no help at all. I haven't been able to find information on how to make it work as advertised online either, but at least Google doesn't blame me for the faulty product.

reviews.title: Un-binds Constantly...
reviews.text: It wont update, most of the watch faces don't download correctly. It decides on it's own when to un-bind from blu tooth. The only reason i'm not returning is because it's comfortable and it tracks my steps and tells time. Literally the only things I use. However, you do not receive messages, you miss calls, and it struggles to bind to your phone. I have had to re-install the app numerous times. When i complained to the online chat. they suggested re-installing the app. That was their only answer to any questions. Doesn't seem like anyone knows really what's going on. either way, if you want to "phone/messages" part of the watch, i highly suggest shopping for a different one.

reviews.title: Face size is great for reading it without glasses
reviews.text: I like being able to see the face and read it without my glasses. I didn't turn on any of the special features like phone, pictures, messaging, etc; mainly because it's made in China, but I'm sure I'm giving away info anyway. Unlike the Fitbit, this doesn't have a way to list and track your food intake and glucose readings. I bought it inorder to read the time, steps, etc, without glasses.

reviews.title: Seems to work pretty well for the most part
reviews.text: Setup was easy with a quick app download and it paired with my iPhone 12 without any drama. Biometric tracking seems to work reasonably well with the exception of heart rate, which seems to always read 70 no matter what I'm doing. I'm not too crazy about the aesthetic of the watch, it just looks kind of cheap to me. I got this mainly to use as a health tracking tool and to tell time, not to make and receive calls/texts with, so I haven't setup any of those features yet and may never get around to doing so. So i can't comment on ease of use for any of those features. I did like that there are quite a lot of different watch face designs that can be applied and without any additional purchase. For the price of around $40, this is a pretty smokin' deal for a smart watch, I think.

reviews.title: Not bad for the price
reviews.text:
I’ve tried a few different affordable watches and my biggest issue has been the charging port. They have trouble staying connected with tiny pins and no magnetic pull. So going into this I’m more critical.
Upside is there is definitely a better charging system. It holds in place nicely even though it’s a pretty small pin area it is easier to charge.
The screen does have low sensitivity response. When pairing it it took 3 tries to get it recognize I had tapped the screen.
On a good note it’s reading well and has some great features like heart rate, oxygen, and sleep. Everything lined up well with my normal statistics so I feel it’s pretty accurate.
There’s a variety of images to choose from so you can customize the screen however you would like.
It’s very comfortable. The band holds like a premium watch band. It has a large face that is classy. Aesthetically it’s very nice.
Overall for an affordable smart watch it’s a decent option.

reviews.title: Okay tracker
reviews.text: For the price, it is an okay tracker.  The microphone on the watch is not of great quality as I was told sounded muffled on it.  It does have a lot of features and was quite easy to use but nothing about it made it stand out from the other hundreds of smart watches out on the market.

reviews.title: Cute watch is all I can say
reviews.text: Initially the watch was quick to charge and holds the charge for a couple of days.  The functions were easy to use, and a lot to choose from for the activities I do.  I mainly track my steps, distance, duration, heart rate.  It worked well for a few weeks, then I took it swimming for a few laps in the pool.  That was a mistake, battery stopped working (won't charge anymore).  Sad, I really liked the watch, it's cute but the battery died.

reviews.title: Great Smart watch for the essentials
reviews.text:
I was looking for a good smart watch for my mother. She was a Fitbit user but her Fitbit died after years of use and wanted something with a similar feel.
This watch really worked out for her.
It's got a great screen and a good amount of apps to provide essential uses.
It's able to work with iOS and Android.
It's able to get notifications from apps and make phone calls.
Not a bad product at all.

title: B-Great Ankle Band for Men and Women Compatible with Fitbit Flex 2/One/Zip/Charge 2 3/Alta HR or Garmin Vivofit/2/3/4/JR Fitness Tracker
description: Comfortable to wear activity tracker around your ankle - keep tracks of ALL your steps while exercising (like on a treadmill, or pushing a grocery cart, or ridding a bike, etc). The band attach a mesh pouch to hold your tracker and that closed with a touch fastener inside, so you don't have to worry about your tracker slipping out any more.Very soft, light-weight, even you don't notice it's there. Easy to use with snaps closure and adjust size. 100% man made, very durable. At present, our ankle band compatible with the following models: Fitbit Flex, Fitbit Flex 2Fitbit OneFitbit ZipFitbit Charge 2 or Charge 3 (Not Charge or Charge HR) Fitbit Alta, Fitbit Alta HR Fitbit Inspire, Fitbit Inspire HRGarmin Vivofit/ 2/ 3/ 4/ JRMisfit Shine, Misfit Shine 2Sony SmartbandOther bullet style activity tracker..... How to detect your heart rate with this ankle band?To track heart rate, just simply flip over your fitness tracker and put it into the mesh pouch, make sure the heart rate monitor face up to your ankle skin, it will detect your heart rate through the small mesh holes and works fine.
features: Allow you wear activity tracker around your ankle and get more accurate steps
Compatible with Fitbit, Garmin, Misfit, Sony or other bullet style activity tracker
Soft and comfortable fit, does not irritates skins, easy to use with snap closure
Keep your tracker secure in the mesh pouch that closed with a touch fastener
Package inlcude only one piece ankle band, not include tracker or other parts

reviews.title: Very accurate on counting steps
reviews.text: I'm happy I found this! In my opinion, it is way more accurate than wearing my Fitbit on my wrist. I've gone from thinking I was a snail and not moving most days to a walking whiz! Just keep in mind if you are out in public with pants that don't cover it, folks may look at you. It looks a little like an electronic monitoring bracelet. Which is fun to me. Let them wonder what I've been up to.....

reviews.title: Heart rate not detectable most of the time
reviews.text: It's nice for working out. But it does not track heart rate with a darn. Lights facing in though the mesh tight on the skin and nadda. A big disappointment. Directly on the skin, adjusted all around. Charge 3

reviews.title: Didn't count steps
reviews.text: I used this for a few weeks because I didn't feel my band was counting steps when I was shopping or carrying something.  I was very surprised to see that after a full day out-and-about I had under 300 steps on the counter.  That's roughly what I would normally have when I got up to get my morning coffee.  I tried re-adjusting and tried the other leg, but got the same result.  I'm back to keeping my tracker on my wrist.  Disappointed.

reviews.title: Comfortable and Lightweight
reviews.text: I love this! It is exactly what I need to help me keep moving and to accurately register steps as I work to increase my activity! The band is light and very comfortable. I hardly even notice that I have it on. The band does have some stretch to it which helps in making it easy to put on.

reviews.title: Works great!
reviews.text: This band has helped me continue to get my steps when I am watching and carrying grandchildren, pushing a stroller or grocery cart, doing a lot of carrying around the house...I love it!  It is comfortable and secure!  With four snaps I can't imagine how you could lose it!

title: FOXIONO Smart Watch for Android and iOS Phones, Fitness Watch ，1.83" Touchscreen Smartwatch, Heart Rate Monitor, Blood Pressure, Blood Oxygen Tracking, Heart Rate Monitor Watch…
features: ⌚ Smart watch contains more practical features, fitness tracker (pedometer/calorie/distance), heart rate monitor, please note, only as life application, not as medical data, sleep monitor, 25 exercise modes, IP68 waterproof, blood oxygen monitor, call notification, SMS and APP messaging (WhatsAPP, Facebook...)

⌚ 【 1.83" HD Full Touch Screen and Custom Watch Face】Our fitness watch have a 1.83 inch TFT-LCD touch screen, make it easy to operate and easily check detailed data reports. Your message notifications can also be displayed straight to your device screen. You can easily operate and view sports data. There are a variety of fitness watch face styles to choose from in the Keep Health app, and you can also customize the watch face with your favorite photos.

⌚ 【Smart Notificationsd and heart rate monitor watch】Stay connected with calls, text, other app notifications, and convenient tools like timer, stopwatch, wrist sense, DND mode, find phone, sedentary reminder, music control, and weather display. Impressive 7-day battery life between a 2h flash charge, you can relax and enjoy more than a week of travel or work

⌚ 【 All-Day Activity Health Tracking The fitness watch 】 Our sport watch supports 25 sports modes(Walking, Running, Cycling, Rugby, Yoga, Soccer, basketball, etc). Fitness tracker watch accurately records all-day activities such as steps, distance walked, calories burned and active minutes, motivating you to reach your exercise goal with real-time statistics on the fitness smartwatch. Stay motivated all day!

⌚ 【 IP68 Waterproof Smart Watch for Android IOS Phones 】Waterproof smartwatch is designed with IP68 waterproof rating, you can wear it when washing hands or running in the rain with it. Our watch for men is compatible with iOS 9.0/Android 4.0 or above smartphones. Thefitness watch is exquisite, lightweight and soft, being very comfortable for wearing💕

reviews.title: Bright, easy, but BP isn't that accurate
reviews.text: The watch face is very clear, and the touch screen options are easy to find.  The blood pressure reads much higher than an actual cuff.  It also needs some more realistic watch faces; the watch face selection is high on gimicky looks and low on more conservative, watch-like looks.

title: UMIDIGI UFit Pro(40mm) Smart Watch Alexa Built-in,Fitness Tracker with Heart Rate, SpO2 and Sleep Monitor, 5ATM Waterproof HD Color Touchscreen for Men Women Compatible with iPhone & Android
features: 🍀【Alexa Built-in】You can talk to Amazon Alexa on your UMIDIGI UFit Pro Smart Watch. Ask questions,play music,set alarms and timers, check the weather, control your smart home devices, and more just by speaking to your watch.

🍀【Two Sizes of HD Color Touch Screen】UFit Pro 44mm have 1.69'' large HD color full screen,UFit Pro 40mm have 1.5'' mini HD color full screen.They have the same function.and ensures smooth operations and 240*280 resolution for you.

🍀【Your personal health tracker】Blood Oxygen(SpO2) Monitor,High Precision Real-Time Heart Rate Monitor,Sleep Monitor,Stress Level Monitor and Health Reminders.All are key indicators of your overall wellness. They Track Your Health Smarter.UMIDIGI UFit Pro smartwatch also automatically records daily steps, Distance Counter，calories burned, active minutes,and understand your physical state

🍀【 Smart Assistant for You】Now you can take a photo on the watch by the remote shutter, or control music playback. Message notifications can be read directly on the watch. And more other features like the Weather forecast, Flashlight, Find My Phone, Alarm are available to let you have a more convenient life.

🍀【Long-lasting Battery Life & 5ATM Waterproof】UMIDIGI UFit Pro provides long battery life of up to 15 days with daily use. Keep tracking your workout data wherever you go.The Smart Watch is designed with 5ATM waterproof rating(50m waterproof), so no need to worry when you wear it while swimming or sweating out.

reviews.title: I wanted to love it
reviews.text: It really isn't the watches fault that I returned it.  It did all it said it would do, the Alexa feature worked, the heart rate monitor worked , the picture was bright, among other things. I didn't like that if you use the timer ( which I use frequently at my job) you can't go back to the main screen, if you do it turns off the timer, but you can use Alexa as a timer and don't have to stay on the same screen so there is a work around. I was also dissapointed with the fact there were only 4 face choices.  But, the reason I returned it is that my phone is older and it wears down my battery quickly and was causing cpu (?) overload warnings.

reviews.title: Solid watch
reviews.text:
Watch came well packaged and was easy to set up and pair using the provided instructions on the item page. It was made well and kept good time. I have a Motorola phone and it would not stay paired with my phone. The sleep feature never seemed to work because it was constantly disconnecting from the app. It may be a good choice for someone else, but I returned it.

The battery life was excellent and it was a well constructed and comfortable watch

title: Smart Watch for Men Women (Make/Answer Call), Fitness Tracker for Android and iOS Phones with Heart Rate Sleep Tracking for Health, Waterproof Large Screen Smartwatches Monitor Pedometer(Black)
description: 2022 upgraded smartwatch (answering/making calls) to start a smart life!
features: 💧【Fitness Tracker Watch for Men and Ladies 】: The health smart watch can set more than 20 sports modes: running,

reviews.title: Junk
reviews.text: Could not get anything to connect to phone but camera! Had two other ones in the past they connected quick and easy. There’s a reason it was so cheap! Sent it back!

title: Samsung Galaxy Watch 4 Classic 42mm Smartwatch with ECG Monitor Tracker for Health Fitness Running Sleep Cycles GPS Fall Detection Bluetooth US Version, Black (Renewed)
description: This pre-owned or refurbished product has been professionally inspected and tested to work and look like new. How a product becomes part of Amazon Renewed, your destination for pre-owned, refurbished products: A customer buys a new product and returns it or trades it in for a newer or different model. That product is inspected and tested to work and look like new by Amazon-qualified suppliers. Then, the product is sold as an Amazon Renewed product on Amazon. If not satisfied with the purchase, renewed products are eligible for replacement or refund under the Amazon Renewed Guarantee.
features: 1.2 "(30.5mm) w/ Rotating Bezel, Super AMOLED, 396 x 396 pixels (~330 ppi density), 16M Colors, CPU: Dual-Core 1.15GHz, 247mAh Battery, Android Wear OS, One UI Watch 3, Bluetooth, Wi-Fi
The Galaxy Watch4 Classic uses red LEDs and infrared rays to calculate your SpO2 levels or the amount of oxygen in your blood.
Glass front (Gorilla Glass DX+), MIL-STD-810G compliant, 50m water-resistant (IP68), Compatible with standard 20mm straps, ECG certified, Blood pressure monitor
5 ATM / IP68 The water and dust resistance rating, Military-grade durability, Larger screen but lighter and thinner for more comfort.
Compatible with Android Phones Running Android 6.0 and Above. NOT compatible with iOS.

reviews.title: Just the works.
reviews.text: It didn't have any manual or anything written enclosed to help a novice like me get it charged up and working.  I'm waiting for my son to arrive for setup help.  It looks like new with no scratches, no blemishes or other indications of wear.

reviews.title: Great Watch
reviews.text: I bought this watch I’m a refurbished deal during Amazon days. I never thought I would find myself wearing this type of watch, but I absolutely find myself loving it. I took this deal hesitantly but find that I can’t tell the difference between this and a new watch. The watch arrived absolutely flawless and looked like a brand new watch. I really see no difference between It and Products that I have received new. I reviewed other watches and other types of watches before purchasing the watch 4, But I am truly glad that I made the purchase that I did. I’m also very satisfied with the refurbish condition I got from Amazon.

reviews.title: Returned bad battery life
reviews.text:
Never really got to use the watch. Battery in normal mode only lasted about 10 hours. In battery saver mode the battery level dropped 10% in 7 hours with the watch turned off and sitting on a dresser not being worn.
Not worth the cost in my eyes.

reviews.title: this is the worst watch I have ever had
reviews.text:
First off, this watch will not last more then 4 hours.
had it on the charger for about 4 days, and still goes empty in about 4 hours
